stories.title: A ferry carrying 116 people capsizes off Guyana, with 67 rescued as search continues
stories.summary:
A ferry carrying 116 people capsizes off Guyana, with 67 rescued as search continues. A ferry carrying 116 passengers and crew capsized in Guyana, with 67 people rescued so far, authorities said Sunday.

Al Jazeera: Guyana ferry with 116 people on board capsizes; more than 50 rescued.

Al Jazeera reports that rescue operations for remaining passengers and crew ongoing, authorities say.

stories.title: Tate Brothers Slapped With 38 New Charges In Expanding Sex Trafficking Case
stories.summary:
Tate Brothers Slapped With 38 New Charges In Expanding Sex Trafficking Case. British prosecutors have issued 38 new criminal charges against Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan after the pair were arrested in Miami on Saturday by federal officials on rape charges and an outstanding warrant in the United Kingdom.

The additional charges pave the way for possible extradition proceedings across the pond as the pair now ... Al Jazeera: Tate brothers arrested in the US.

Al Jazeera reports that video shows the moment controversial influencer brothers Andrew Tate and Tristan Tate were arrested in Miami, US.

stories.title: Iran attacks US Gulf allies, causing a fire at Kuwait power plant
stories.summary:
Iran attacks US Gulf allies, causing a fire at Kuwait power plant. Kuwait and Bahrain report several rounds of Iranian missile, drone attacks; sirens reportedly sound in Jordan.

stories.title: FTC chair Lina Khan warns AI could 'turbocharge' fraud and scams
stories.summary: FTC chair Lina Khan warns AI could 'turbocharge' fraud and scams. Artificial intelligence tools such as ChatGPT could lead to a "turbocharging" of consumer harms including fraud and scams, and the US government has substantial authority to crack down on AI-driven consumer harms under existing law, members of the Federal Trade Commission said Tuesday.

stories.summary:

stories.summary:
Trump urges Iran’s addition to Russian sanctions bill: ‘What Lindsey wanted’. President Donald Trump urged Republicans to add Iran to the Russian sanctions bill, invoking former Sen. Lindsey Graham’s memory in support.

In a Sunday morning post on Truth Social, writing hours after hitting Iran in the eighth consecutive night of major strikes by U.S. Central Command forces, the president sought to hit Tehran with another […]. Washington Times: Trump calls for Iran to be added to Russia sanctions bill.

stories.title: Asylum seekers sent to Nauru by Australia say they are going hungry
stories.summary: Asylum seekers sent to Nauru by Australia say they are going hungry. According to a survey of 78 asylum seekers on Nauru, 99 per cent said they were regularly skipping meals because they could not afford to buy enough food, while more than three quarters said they rarely had access to free, clean drinking water.

stories.title: Smart glasses are deeply creepy. Why are celebrities like Kylie Jenner endorsing them?
stories.summary:
Smart glasses are deeply creepy. Why are celebrities like Kylie Jenner endorsing them?.

Meta touts safety features – but for women, the dangers of these recording devices are obvious Imagine if every time you left the house, you couldn’t be sure that the stranger you met at a bar – or even the person walking by you in the street – wasn’t secretly recording you.

It sounds like something out of a Black Mirror episode, but let’s face it, the era of wearable.

stories.title: ‘We noticed a login from a new device’: the message from fraudsters targeting your X account
stories.summary:
‘We noticed a login from a new device’: the message from fraudsters targeting your X account. They are out to steal your password to commit further fraud such as crypto scams or phishing attacks You have had an X account for years, since it was known as Twitter.

When an email arrives about a new login from a location nowhere near where you live, alarm bells begin to ring.

“We noticed a login to your account from a new device. Was this you?” the email asks.

C.

links.headline: ‘We noticed a login from a new device’: the message from fraudsters targeting your X account

stories.title: La Union intensifies water quality monitoring at 20 ecotourism sites
stories.summary:
La Union intensifies water quality monitoring at 20 ecotourism sites. CALASIAO, Pangasinan — The La Union provincial government is conducting regular water quality monitoring in rivers, waterfalls, and other inland waters at 20 ecotourism sites to help ensure public safety and protect the province’s natural resources.

The initiative, led by the Provincial Government–Environment and Natural Resources Office (PG-ENRO), is part of the province’s environmental management.

stories.summary:

LAOGA CITY — Hundreds of persons with disabilities (PWDs), their families, advocates and government agencies gathered here on Sunday for a fun run and walk to promote inclusivity and physical fitness in celebration of National Disability Rights Week.

stories.title: Bukidnon road cracks prompt motorist safety alert
stories.summary:
Bukidnon road cracks prompt motorist safety alert.

VALENCIA CITY, Bukidnon — Authorities have urged motorists to exercise caution when passing through Poblacion, Damulog, Bukidnon, after a nearly 100-meter crack developed on a secondary two-lane concrete road following the July 17 landslide in Sitio Marahan, Marilog District, Davao City.

stories.summary: Apple and Google ordered to purge ‘nudify’ apps from App Stores. In letters sent to Apple and Google, San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu said that both companies have long been aware that they are hosting apps in violation of state law.
